claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics: **Do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Now I need to understand a bit more about Alex's situation.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 20 years old, this is important for determining eligibility, as Child Benefit normally applies to children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, I'm afraid you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for him. Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Once a child turns 20, or if they're 16-19 and not in approved education or training, Child Benefit stops.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. 

The reason is that Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for:
- Children under 16, OR
- Young people aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training

Once a young person reaches 20 years old, Child Benefit eligibility ends regardless of their circumstances.

If you have any other questions or if Alex's circumstances change (though at 20, eligibility has ended), please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit normally qualifies for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 and not in approved education or training, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility ends at age 20, or earlier (at 16-19) if the young person is not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit normally qualifies for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 and not in approved education or training, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility ends at age 20, or earlier (at 16-19) if the young person is not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
